pattern: A fixed-size sliding window over the array, tracking a running sum and a frequency map for the distinct-element count, turns 'best sum among length-k windows with at least m distinct values' into a single linear pass.
hints: The subarray length is fixed at k, so slide a window of exactly that size across nums instead of trying every possible start and end.
Maintain the window's running sum and a count of how many distinct values currently sit in it (a frequency map tells you when a value's count drops to zero or rises from zero).
Whenever the window has at least m distinct values, it is a candidate -- keep a running maximum sum across all such windows, and default to 0 if none ever qualify.

edge_inputs.description: window size equal to the whole array
m equals k, every element in the window must be distinct
k = 1, single-element windows always have exactly one distinct value
no window of the required length ever reaches m distinct values
the highest-sum window doesn't have enough distinct values, a smaller-sum window must win instead
all elements distinct, sum is maximized by the largest contiguous run

stress.description: random array near the max length with a mix of repeated and unique values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 20000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    m = rng.randint(1, k)
    return [nums, m, k]
